texto: As if the jealousy of a lover could ever compete with the ferocity of a mother.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Sarah A. Denzil: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “As if the jealousy of a lover could ever compete with the ferocity of a mother.” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, fiction, jealousy pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: The mother is always at fault. so is the wife, really. Women are supposed to control men, isn't that how it goes? what's that saying again: 'behind every great man is a great woman.' we're supposed to be the ones holding them back. forget having our own lives. forget our own careers and loves and losses. we're the matriarchs.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Sarah A. Denzil que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “The mother is always at fault. so is the wife, really. Women are supposed to control men, isn't t…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, thriller, supposed, com foco em luto e reflexao.

texto: No one wants to believe they are weak, but we all have weakness inside us. We have strength, too, but there are times when the circumstances in our lives are so overwhelming that we easily succumb to that weakness
explicacao: Citação de Sarah A. Denzil sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “No one wants to believe they are weak, but we all have weakness inside us. We have strength, too,…” articula tempo, weakness, weakness-and-strength em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como weakness-and-strength.
